mnemonics: PIPES: Pearson's Information Processing & Evaluation System (2011)
keyPoints: Pearson 2011 introduced the concept of 'information foraging' to describe how users seek information.
The paper emphasizes evaluating IR systems by how well they support user tasks, not just by traditional metrics like precision/recall.
It bridges the gap between classic algorithmic IR and user-centered interactive IR.
commonMistakes: Confusing Pearson 2011 with the Pearson correlation coefficient used in statistics.
Focusing only on the system's algorithm and ignoring the user's search strategies and context.
Assuming the primary contribution is a new technical model rather than a new evaluation philosophy.
